name: tui-presentation
description: TUI/chat presentation for agents: seven-shape vocabulary (framed card, sub-framed grid, ruled card, column-ruled, pipeline, state diagram, no-frame list), document structure with cordoning, and build discipline for alignment and overflow. Designed for rendering contexts where markdown is unreliable for document structure while semantic markdown remains useful for code, file links, URLs, and technical tokens.

interface.shortDescription: Seven-shape vocabulary, document structure, and build discipline for monospace chat output
interface.longDescription: Produce readable, aligned Unicode presentations for design, architecture, comparisons, tradeoffs, UI mockups, data flows, state machines, and multi-section explanations in rendering contexts where markdown is unreliable for document structure. Teaches seven shapes, document structure with cordoning, alignment mechanics, overflow policy, and semantic markdown boundaries for code blocks, inline technical tokens, file links, and URLs. Strong user preference — reach for this whenever explaining, comparing, or proposing design in chat or TUI output.
defaultPrompt: Present this clearly for a monospace chat or TUI surface.
